original: 18 Rabbit, ruler of powerful Maya city Copán, installs Cauac Sky as ruler of his vassal city of Quiriguá, who later famously captures and beheads 18 Rabbit in 738
Battle of Garigliano: Spanish army under Gonzalo Fernández de Córdoba defeats French forces in Italy, giving Spain control of the Kingdom of Naples
Portuguese under Francisco de Almeida, attack, pillage and burn the Muslim trading city of Dabul, Sultanate of Bijapur (modern India)
St Jacob's Church burns after being hit by lightning
Isabella of Poland and King Ferdinand of Austria sign Treaty of Gyalu
Danish student and future astronomer Tycho Brache loses part of his nose in a sword duel over who is the better mathematician (he wore a prosthetic nose the rest of his life)
Prosper Jolyot de Crébillon's play "Idoménée" premieres in Paris
Great Alliance occupies Gent
British troops occupy Savannah, Georgia
1st nautical almanac in US published by Samuel Stearns, Boston
French Revolution: The Assembly of Notables is convoked
The USS Constitution under the command of Captain William Bainbridge, captures the HMS Java off the coast of Brazil after a three hour battle
British burn Buffalo, New York during War of 1812
Canadian militia destroy Caroline, a US steamboat docked at Buffalo
Steam-powered threshing machine patented in Winthrop, Maine
King and Grand Duke Willem II installs Order of Eikenkroon
Texas is admitted as the 28th state of the Union
Gas lights 1st installed at White House (Polk's administration)
Emma Snodgrass is arrested in Boston for wearing pants
The first British seagoing iron-clad warship, HMS Warrior is launched
Bowling ball invented
1st telegraph ticker used by a brokerage house, Groesbeck & Co, New York
11 passenger cars crash in a ravine near Ashtabula, Ohio, 92 die
Edison patents "transmission of signals electrically" (radio)
Dr L S Jameson begins failed raid on Johannesburg
British fleet seizes German Imperial Mail Steamer Bundesrath accused of carrying contraband
General Viljoen surprise attack British garrison to Helvetia
French Equatorial Africa separates into Gabon, Chad and Ubangi-Shari (Central African Republic)
Stanley Cup, Montreal Arena, Westmount, Quebec: Montreal Wanderers beat New Glasgow (NS), 7-2 for 17-5 aggregate challenge series victory
Patent granted for a 4-wheel automobile brake, Clintonville, Wisconsin
Mongolia gains independence from the Qing dynasty
Proclamation restores "Dei Gratia" from Canada's coins
San Francisco Symphony forms
First movie serial "The Adventures of Kathlyn" premieres in Chicago
The Netherlands and Venezuela recover diplomatic relations
Yugoslav government bans communist party
Dutch Constitution proclaimed
Revised Netherlands Law proclaims suffrage
Vatican puts French fascist Charles Maurras' work on the index
Victoria (1107) beat NSW (221 & 230) by an innings 656 runs

Fred Newton (27) becomes the first to swim the entire length of the Mississippi River from Ford Dam, Minnesota, to New Orleans, Louisiana; he covers 1,826 miles over 176 days, spending 742 hours in the water
Identification of heavy water publicly announced, HC Urey
Victoria score 7 for 435 in second innings to beat NSW
Federico Garcia Lorca's "Yerma" premieres in Madrid
First collegiate basketball doubleheader is played at Madison Square Garden, NYC; New York University beats Notre Dame, 25-18; Westminster edges St. John's, 37-33
Japan renounces Wash Naval Treaty of 1922 and London Treaty of 1930
Constitution of Ireland (Irish: Bunreacht na hÉireann) is enacted and Irish free state is named Eire
Pan Am starts service between San Francisco and Auckland, New Zealand
Construction on Lake Washington Floating Bridge, Seattle, begins
First flight of the Consolidated B-24 Liberator bomber prototype
Belgian Walloon Nazi collaborator Léon Degrelle sentenced to death
GeneralEisenhower's train returns to Versailles
Ship carrying Jewish immigrants driven away from Palestine
Canada recognizes Israel
US State Department announces work on placing objects into Earth orbit
1st UHF television station operating regular basis Bridgeport, Connecticut
Hungary nationalized its industries
1st transistorized hearing aid offered for sale (Elmsford NY)
Kingdom of Netherlands, with Netherlands & Netherlands Antilles as autonomous parts, comes into being
NHL officials wear new vertically striped black-and-white sweaters for the first time in Montreal Canadiens' 5-2 win over Toronto Maple Leafs
TV soap "Young Dr Malone" debuts
Saul Levitt's "Andersonville Trial" premieres in NYC
CBS purchases NFL TV rights for 1966-68 at $18.8 million per year
Pirate Radio Phoenix, 1st transmission (Worcester, Massachusetts)
Star Trek's "Trouble With Tribbles" 1st airs
Turkish-Cypriot government forms in Cyprus
Israeli commandos destroy 13 Lebanese airplanes
Occupational Safety and Health Act (United States) signed into law by President Richard M. Nixon
"Aguirre, the Wrath of God" film written and directed by Werner Herzog, starring Klaus Kinski premieres in West Germany
Eastern Tristar Jumbo Jet crashes near Everglades killing 101
Life magazine ceases publication
President of Sinn Féin Ruairi O Bradaigh is arrested and held under new legislation in Republic of Ireland
Test Cricket debut of Jeff Thomson and Max Walker v Pakistan at MCG
11 killed, 75 hurt by terrorist bomb at LaGuardia Airport in NYC
Ronald Ribman's "Cold Storage" premieres in NYC
Spain constitution goes into effect
Test Cricket debut of Allan Robert Border, v England at the MCG
Red Army beats NY Islanders 3-2 at Nassau Coliseum
Shuttle STS-1 moves from Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) to Launch Complex 39A

Ballon d'Or: German 1-2-3 with Bayern Munich forward Karl-Heinz Rummenigge taking title of Europe's best football player for the 2nd straight year; beats teammate Paul Breitner and Barcelona midfielder Bernd Schuster
Australian cricket batsman Graham Yallop completes a painstaking 268 (716 mins) v Pakistan in the drawn 4th Test in Melbourne

US formally notifies UNESCO that it will withdraw from the organization on the Jan 1, 1985, in protest over perceived anti-Western bias, efforts to restrict press freedom and wasteful management methods
5th United Negro College Fund
NHL St. Louis Blues launch 27 shots in 1 period during 5-1 loss to visiting New York Islanders; NY goaltender Kelly Hrudey finishes game with 47 saves
Ballon d'Or: Milan's Dutch midfielder Ruud Gullit wins best football player in Europe award ahead of Paulo Futre (Porto/Atlético Madrid) and Real Madrid striker Emilio Butragueño
US Food and Drug Administration first approves the use of Prozac (fluoxetine hydrochloride) to treat depression
Soviet Red Army Team edges NY Islanders, 3-2 at Nassau Coliseum
Toronto’s Ed Olczyk and Gary Leeman score goals just 4 seconds apart in the 3rd period, tying the NHL record for fastest 2 goals by one team; Leafs beat the Quebec Nordiques, 6-5.
Victorian Post Office Museum in Australia closes
Jane Pauley says goodbye to NBC's "Today" show
Riots break-out after Hong Kong decides to forcibly repatriate Vietnamese refugees.
Wayne Gretzkyand Martina Navratilova are named Athletes of the Decade by the Associated Press
12th United Negro College Fund "Parade of Stars" telethon, hosted by Lou Rawls airs from Aquarius Theatre in Hollywood, and The Apollo Theatre in Harlem, NYC
Boeing 747-200F freight plane of China Airlines crashes into mountain shortly after takeoff from Chiang Kai-shek International Airportat Taipei, Taiwan, killing 5-member crew
Governor Cuomo grants Jean Harris (Scarsdale Diet Dr killer) clemency
Todd Bridges arrested for transporting methamphetamine (speed)
B737-400 flies into a mountain at Edremit East Turkey, 54 killed
Bangladesh government of Zia resigns
Last Dutch electro-magnetic telephone exchange shuts down
Hong Kong begins slaughtering all its chickens to prevent bird flu
Orville Lynn Majors, 36, arrested for many deaths under his care
Russia signs agreement to build a $3B nuclear power plant in China
Leaders of the Khmer Rouge apologize for the 1970s genocide in Cambodia that claimed over a million people
Mesa Redonda shopping center fire, Lima, Peru, at least 291 killed.
American boxer Steve Cunningham defends his IBF cruiserweight title for the first time with a 12th-round TKO of local favourite Marco Huck in Bielefeld, Germany
200 people are executed by the Syrian army in Homs
21 security personnel are killed by Pakistani Taliban near Peshawar
5 people are killed in a Tupolev Tu-204 plane crash in Moscow
16 people are killed and 40 are wounded by a suicide bomb attack at Volgograd-1 railway station, Russia
Chaparrastique volcano erupts in El Salvador
Syrian Observatory for Human Rights says barrel bombs on Aleppo have killed 517 people since 15th December
Ebola epidemic in Guinea declared over by WHO, 2,500 died over 2 years
40 militants killed in Giza and El-Arish by Egyptian police after attack on a tourist bus the day before killed four
85th Orange Bowl: #1 Alabama beats #4 Oklahoma, 45-34
Record lowest temperature of -111C registered by American satellite Noaa-20 in the western Pacific at top of large storm
Milwaukee Bucks break NBA record for most 3-pointers made by a team in a single game with 29 in a dominant 144-97 victory over the Heat in Miami
Social medial personalityAndrew Tatearrested in Romania on charges of sex trafficking and rape, following an exchange with Greta Thunberg over Twitter
South Korean Jeju Air plane crashes and explodes, trying to land without landing gear at Muan International Airport, killing 179 out of 181 on board
English Archbishop of Canterbury,Thomas Becketassassinated before the high altar of Canterbury Cathedral by four knights
Charles V, King of Spain and Holy Roman Emperor, buried in El Escorial, San Lorenzo de El Escorial, Spain
Dutch painterJan Vermeerbecomes a member of the Guild of Saint Luke for painters in Delft
Franz Liszt's symphonic poem "Hunnenschlacht (The Battle of the Huns)" premieres in Weimar
Battle of Chichasaw Bayou: confederate armies defeat GeneralWilliam T. Sherman
Scott Joplincopyrights "The Entertainer" and several other piano rag compositions with the US Copyright Office
Chinese revolutionarySun Yat-senis elected the 1st President of the Republic of China
Mackenzie Kingis sworn in as the 10th Prime Minister of Canada
Dutch colonial authorities arrestSukarnoand other key PNI leaders in Java
Fred AstaireandGinger Rogers' 1st movie together "Flying Down to Rio", directed by Thornton Freeland, premieres in NYC
"Sons of the Desert" film directed by William A. Seiter starringStan LaurelandOliver Hardyis released
New York Yankees owner Jacob Ruppert refuses to release future Baseball Hall of Fame sluggerBabe Ruthto manage the Cincinnati Reds
Lou Theszbeats Everett Marshall in St Louis, to win NWA World heavyweight title - youngest ever

Montreal right wingMaurice Richardscores twice in Canadiens' 5-4 loss to Chicago Black Hawks to record his 100th NHL career goal; reaches mark in 145 games, then fastest in history; since broken by Mike Bossy, 100 goals in 129 games
Barbra Streisand's 1st recording "You'll Never Know" at age 13
British driverGraham Hillwins the South African Grand Prix at Prince George Circuit in a BRM; takes out his first F1 World Drivers Championship by 12 points from ScotsmanJim Clark
"Thunderball", 4th James Bond film starringSean Conneryand Claudine Auger premieres in Tokyo, Japan
The Supremesrelease single "My World is Empty Without You"
AFL Championship, Shea Stadium, NYC: New York Jets beat Oakland Raiders 27-23;Joe Namath3 TD passes sparks Jets to win
Ballon d'Or: Bayern Munich strikerGerd Müllerwins award for best European football player ahead of West Ham United defender Bobby Moore and Cagliari forward Luigi Riva
Shah of IranMohammad Reza Pahlaviasks Shapour Bahktiar to form a civilian government
American college football coachPaul "Bear" Bryantends his career with University of Alabama (323 wins)
Jamaican Post issues commemorative postage stamp featuring reggae singerBob Marley
Indian cricket opening batsmanSunil Gavaskarscores an unbeaten 236 in drawn 6th Test v West Indies at Chennai; his 4th Test double century and 30th century, surpassingDon Bradman's record of 29
Indian Prime MinisterRajiv Gandhiclaims victory in parliamentary elections

Wayne Gretzkyscores his NHL career 32nd hat trick & adds 3 assists in Edmonton's 6-3 win over Detroit Red Wings for his 100th point in just 35 games; 2nd fastest in history - recorded 100 points in 34 games in 1983
Václav Havelis selected to be president of Czechoslovakia by the Federal Assembly shortly after the Velvet Revolution
Courtney Lovesues doctors for leaking news of her methadone treatment
Tom Bradysets a then-NFL record, throwing his 50th touchdown pass, and the New England Patriots become the first team in NFL history to finish the regular season 16-0
7-time world F1 motor racing championMichael Schumachersuffers a serious head injury in a ski accident in the French Alps; his condition still remains unclear
US PresidentBarack Obamaretaliates against Russia for hacking American computer systems and trying to influence the 2016 presidential election by ejecting 35 Russian spies and imposing sanctions
Rashid Khanis named the captain of Afghanistan's T20I team

Treaty of New Echota is signed between the US government and representatives of a minority Cherokee political faction to cede all lands of the Cherokee east of the Mississippi River to the United States
US 7th Cavalry massacre 200+ captive Sioux at Wounded Knee, South Dakota
Worst German air raid on London as over 10,000 bombs including the 1st incendiary bombs are dropped on the city as part of the Blitz
